slides.title: Your body keeps a clock. *Shift work* moves it.
slides.sub: Working against the sun pulls your internal rhythm out of sync. A few small habits can soften the toll.

slides.title: An internal clock
slides.body: Every cell runs on a roughly 24-hour rhythm set by *light and meal timing*. Night shifts ask it to flip overnight.

slides.title: Misalignment adds up
slides.body: Eating and working in the dark can desync that clock. Long-term shift work *is linked to* higher metabolic and heart risk.

slides.title: Use light on purpose
slides.body: Bright light on shift, dark and dim on the way home. Controlled light *can help* anchor your rhythm.

slides.title: Tighten the eating window
slides.body: Front-load food toward your active hours and ease off deep overnight. A steady window *may ease* the metabolic load.

slides.title: Protect the sleep you get
slides.body: Cool, dark, quiet, and consistent timing matter most. Guarding daytime sleep *can support* recovery between shifts.

slides.title: Bring rhythm to an *odd schedule*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, food, movement and steps across irregular hours, turning them into one daily Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Shift work asks your body to live against its own clock. With a little structure, you can soften the toll. 🌙

Use light on purpose, tighten your eating window, and guard the sleep you get. Small anchors, steadier rhythm. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Shift Work and Your Body Clock: How to Soften the Toll
